channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: SRM Energy Auditors Resign After New Promoters Take Charge
item.description: SRM Energy Limited's secretarial auditors, M/s S. K. Nirankar & Associates, have resigned with immediate effect on March 16, 2026. The move follows a recent takeover and acquisition by new promoters, Umesh Narpatchand Sanghvi and Sapna Sanghvi, who are exploring new firms for auditing services. The company's Audit Committee and Board will appoint new auditors.

item.title: Cian Healthcare Appoints Rachit Malhotra to Lead Governance and Compliance
item.description: Cian Healthcare Limited has appointed Mr. Rachit Malhotra as its new Company Secretary and Chief Compliance Officer, effective March 16, 2026. This appointment comes amidst significant corporate restructuring following insolvency proceedings and aims to reinforce the company's governance framework. Mr. Malhotra brings over a decade of experience from the BFSI sector with a strong background in corporate governance and regulatory liaison.

item.title: ShaliBhadra Finance: Promoter Ayushi Doshi Ups Stake to 1.76% With Share Purchase
item.description: Ayushi Doshi, a director and promoter of ShaliBhadra Finance, purchased 9,000 shares on March 16, 2026. Her stake now totals 5,45,000 shares, or 1.76% of the company. The purchase signals continued promoter confidence in the NBFC's focus on rural and semi-urban markets.

item.title: IREDA Shareholders OK Capital Raise Via QIP to Boost Green Funding
item.description: Indian Renewable Energy Development Agency (IREDA) shareholders have approved a special resolution to raise capital through Qualified Institutions Placement (QIP). The approval, secured via remote e-voting, empowers the company to issue equity shares, bolstering its financial capacity to fund India's growing renewable energy sector. This move is crucial for IREDA to meet its lending mandates and expand its role in green financing.

item.title: ShaliBhadra Finance: Promoter Ayushi Doshi Buys 9,000 Shares, Increasing Stake
item.description: Ayushi Doshi, a promoter group member at ShaliBhadra Finance, bought 9,000 equity shares, raising her stake to 5,45,000 shares (1.76% of total equity). The ₹8.43 lakh transaction, disclosed under SEBI rules, signals continued promoter confidence in the NBFC's vehicle financing business for rural and semi-urban markets.

item.title: IEX to Meet Unifi Capital March 19, 2026; No Price-Sensitive Info Shared
item.description: Indian Energy Exchange (IEX) has scheduled a virtual meeting with UNIFI Capital for March 19, 2026. The company stated that no unpublished price-sensitive information will be shared during this session, as part of its ongoing investor dialogue.

item.title: Dodla Dairy Unit Faces ₹18.11 Cr Tax Demand, Plans Appeal
item.description: Dodla Dairy's wholly-owned subsidiary, HR Food Processing Private Limited, has received an Income Tax order demanding ₹18.11 crore for Assessment Year 2024-25. The company's management disputes the assessment, deeming the demand unsustainable, and intends to file an appeal. This tax demand introduces a potential financial contingency for the subsidiary, pending the outcome of the appeal process.

item.title: NOCIL Invests ₹130 Cr to Expand Rubber Chemicals Output at Dahej Plant
item.description: NOCIL's Board has approved a ₹130 crore investment to expand rubber chemical production at its Dahej, Gujarat plant. This brownfield expansion, featuring backward integration, aims to boost operational efficiency and market standing. Completion is targeted for H1 FY28, strengthening NOCIL's leading position in the sector.

item.title: Indian Market Recovers March 16: Nifty Gains 1.11%, Stocks Hit 52-Week Highs
item.description: Indian equity markets reversed early losses on March 16, 2026, closing higher as the Nifty 50 index gained 1.11% to reach 23,408.80. The recovery saw eighteen stocks, including Aeroflex Industries, CLC Industries, and Gaya Projects, hit new 52-week highs, signaling pockets of strength.

item.title: Cian Healthcare Board Approves Allotment, Pushing Promoter Stake to 95%
item.description: Cian Healthcare Limited's board has approved allotting 23,750,000 equity shares to promoters for ₹23.75 crore. This move, following an NCLT order and approved resolution plan, boosts promoter shareholding to 95% and is a key step in the company's revival after insolvency.

item.title: Minda Corp Shareholders Back Expanded ESOPs for Staff & Group Firms
item.description: Minda Corporation Limited shareholders have overwhelmingly approved four special resolutions related to Employee Stock Option Schemes (ESOPs) via postal ballot. The approvals pave the way for the 'Minda - Employee Stock Option Scheme 2025' and allow Minda to grant options to employees across the company, subsidiaries, and group companies, aiming to boost talent retention and motivation. The company is a leading automotive component manufacturer.

item.title: KIMS Hospitals to Host Investor Meeting as Expansion Fuels Growth
item.description: Krishna Institute of Medical Sciences Limited (KIMS) has scheduled an analyst and institutional investor meeting for March 23, 2026, in Mumbai. This engagement is crucial for transparency and maintaining investor confidence, especially as the company undergoes significant expansion.

item.title: Sun Pharma Stock Dips on FDA Review of Psoriatic Arthritis Drug ILUMYA
item.description: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries' ILUMYA drug for active psoriatic arthritis received acceptance for review by the US FDA, with a decision expected by October 29, 2026. However, Sun Pharma shares fell 1.42% on March 16, 2026. This reaction highlights market worries about competition and ILUMYA's ability to gain traction. The company has a P/E ratio of about 38x and a market value near ₹4.2 trillion.

item.title: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery Lists on Exchanges; Reports ₹23 Cr Profit
item.description: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery has successfully listed on the NSE and BSE on March 4, 2026. The company reported a profit after tax of ₹23.11 crore for the quarter ended December 31, 2025. Meanwhile, Dalmia Bharat Sugar has opened a special window for physical share transfer requests. Investors will monitor PNGS Reva's performance post-IPO and potential accounting impacts from new Labour Codes.

item.title: India-US Trade Deal Signing Delayed Over US Tariff Uncertainty
item.description: The signing of a key trade agreement between India and the US has been postponed while the US finalizes a new tariff system. This follows a Supreme Court decision that overturned previous tariffs, leading the US to create a new global tariff framework. India wants to keep its competitive edge and the favorable 18% tariff rate agreed upon in February 2026, which puts it ahead of regional rivals. The delay highlights India's need to secure these terms as US trade policy and tariff rules are still being adjusted and face legal questions.

item.title: MSTR Buys $1.57B Bitcoin Amid Dilution Fears
item.description: MicroStrategy Inc. bolstered its Bitcoin holdings by $1.57 billion, acquiring 22,337 BTC at an average of $70,194 per coin. The significant purchase was financed primarily through the issuance of preferred and common stock, fueling ongoing debates about shareholder dilution. Despite this, Bitcoin's price climbed to approximately $74,000. MicroStrategy's strategy faces scrutiny as its stock has declined year-to-date, though analysts maintain a bullish outlook with high price targets.

item.title: Clean Science R&D Head Resigns, Exit Set for March 2026
item.description: Clean Science and Technology Limited has announced the resignation of its Senior Vice-President of Research and Development, Mr. Kothanda Rama Lakshmanan Pichaandi. His departure, citing personal reasons, will be effective March 14, 2026. This marks a significant leadership change for the company's R&D, which is crucial for its innovation in green chemistry and specialty chemicals.

item.title: Mish Designs Appoints New Independent Director, Changes Tapan Shah's Role
item.description: Mish Designs' board has appointed Kajal Chhatwal as an Additional Independent Director and changed Tapan Shah's role. These moves are intended to boost the board's expertise and governance, awaiting shareholder approval.

item.title: DCB Bank gets RBI nod for Whole Time Director rotation rule change
item.description: DCB Bank announced that the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has approved its proposal to amend its Articles of Association, specifically Article 140B. This amendment will allow Whole Time Directors to be subjected to retirement by rotation, subject to the explicit approval of the Bank's Board of Directors. The RBI's nod, dated March 16, 2026, signifies a shift in governance oversight for key executive positions within the bank.

item.title: EPL Ltd: ₹30 Crore Commercial Papers Mature March 23, 2026
item.description: EPL Limited will repay ₹30 Crore in Commercial Papers on March 23, 2026. This routine debt maturity is a standard financial step for the specialty packaging firm.

item.title: Shristi Infra proposes 3-year MD term for Sunil Jha; FY25 loss shrinks
item.description: Shristi Infrastructure Development Corporation Ltd wants shareholders to re-approve Sunil Jha as Managing Director for a three-year term starting March 4, 2026. The company reported a net loss of ₹10.01 crore for FY2024-25, down from the previous year, on ₹153.68 crore in turnover. Shareholders can vote remotely from March 18 to April 16, 2026.

item.title: Wardwizard Healthcare CFO Mittalben Patel Resigns March 30, 2026
item.description: Mittalben Patel, Chief Financial Officer (CFO) at Wardwizard Healthcare Limited, has resigned, with her final day set for March 30, 2026. The company stated her departure is due to personal growth and new professional opportunities, with no other significant reasons involved. Wardwizard Healthcare will now work to appoint a new CFO to maintain operational continuity.

item.title: India's Gated Communities: $900Bn Spending Hubs Face Scrutiny
item.description: Gated communities in India are rapidly evolving into powerful consumption engines, projected to account for nearly $900 billion in annual spending by fiscal year 2031, a significant surge from approximately $200 billion in 2021. This economic leap is fueled by residents earning five to seven times the national average, with per capita incomes nearing $18,000. These enclaves are becoming dominant hubs for digital commerce, expected to contribute $80-90 billion to India's online retail market, nearly 40% of the total. Brands are leveraging advanced community management platforms for hyper-targeted marketing, unlocking substantial advertising and SaaS opportunities. However, this concentrated affluence raises questions about market sustainability and broader economic inclusivity.

item.title: India Bonds Fall as RBI Stays on Sidelines Amid Oil Surge
item.description: Indian government bonds extended their decline for a third consecutive session, driven by sustained high oil prices and amplified inflation concerns. The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) decision to refrain from additional debt-buying operations, contrary to market expectations, has unnerved traders and is exerting downward pressure on bond valuations. The benchmark 6.48% 2035 bond yield climbed to 6.7059%, reflecting this sentiment. Simultaneously, the Indian rupee reached a record high against the US dollar, underscoring the broad market anxieties stemming from geopolitical instability and its economic fallout.

item.title: Redmax Footwears Shifts Registered Office From Mumbai To New Delhi
item.description: Redmax Footwears Limited has officially relocated its registered office from Mumbai, Maharashtra, to New Delhi. This change, effective March 16, 2026, was approved by the board and received necessary regulatory clearances.

item.title: Tinna Rubber Appoints Abhay Kumar as New CFO, Ravindra Chhabra Retires
item.description: Tinna Rubber And Infrastructure Limited announced a significant leadership transition with CFO Ravindra Chhabra retiring after his tenure. Mr. Abhay Kumar, previously Deputy CFO, has been appointed as his successor, effective March 16, 2026. Mr. Chhabra will continue to support the company in an advisory capacity, ensuring continuity of experience.

item.title: Leela Palaces Hotels asks shareholders to approve 2% ESOP expansion
item.description: Leela Palaces Hotels & Resorts Limited is asking shareholders to approve amendments and ratification of its 2024 Employee Stock Option Scheme. The proposal includes a pool of 6,679,158 stock options, representing 2% of the company's paid-up shares. The company aims to align the scheme with its post-listing status and current market practices to attract and retain talent.

item.title: Fredun Pharma Schedules March 18 Investor Meet on Strategy, Finances
item.description: Fredun Pharmaceuticals will hold an investor meeting in Mumbai on March 18, 2026. Management plans to share a business overview and discuss financial results, providing a forum for stakeholders to discuss operations and future strategy.

item.title: Finolex Industries' IND AA+ Credit Rating Confirmed by India Ratings
item.description: India Ratings and Research has reaffirmed Finolex Industries Limited's credit rating for its bank loan facilities at IND AA+/Stable/IND A1+. The total outstanding bank loan facilities amount to INR 19,163 million, with proposed limits of INR 2,894.5 million withdrawn. This stable rating signifies continued strong creditworthiness and financial stability for the company.

item.title: Power Mech Projects wins ₹709 Cr Adani contract for thermal plant O&M
item.description: Power Mech Projects Limited announced a ₹709.56 crore contract from Adani Infrastructure Management Services for Operations and Maintenance (O&M) at a 5x660MW Thermal Power Plant. The five-year deal, starting April 1, 2026, significantly boosts the company's order book and revenue outlook, highlighting its strong ties with the Adani Group.

item.title: Suraj Industries Raises ₹8.85 Cr, Converts Shares, Issues Forfeiture Warning
item.description: Suraj Industries collected ₹8.85 crore in First Call money on partly paid-up equity shares. The company approved converting 88.48 lakh shares to a higher paid-up value, enabling their listing and trading. A second reminder-cum-forfeiture notice has been issued for the remaining unpaid amount, with a deadline of April 3, 2026.

item.title: CUMI Launches ₹83 Cr Hosur Plant, Doubles Cutting Wheel Capacity
item.description: Carborundum Universal Limited (CUMI) has started production at its new ₹83 crore facility in Hosur, doubling its annual capacity for cutting and grinding wheels to over 90 million units. The plant uses technology from Germany’s DRONCO GmbH and is set up as a Centre of Excellence for domestic and international markets.

item.title: HDFC Bank Issues 1,630,554 Shares to Employees Under Stock Plans
item.description: HDFC Bank has allotted 16,30,554 equity shares to employees upon exercising stock options under its ESOS and RSU schemes. This action increases the bank's total paid-up share capital. The allotment reflects the bank's ongoing strategy to use equity-based compensation for employee retention and motivation.

item.title: Ducon Infratech Director Resigns March 25, Remains CFO
item.description: Ducon Infratechnologies Limited announced that Executive Director and Director Mr. Harish Shetty will step down from his directorial roles effective March 25, 2026, due to other professional commitments. He will continue to serve the company as its Chief Financial Officer, ensuring continuity in financial leadership. This change alters the board composition.

item.title: Five Indian Stocks Bounce From Key Support; Valuations Raise Concerns
item.description: Amid a market rebound, several Indian stocks like Eicher Motors, JSW Steel, and L&T Finance have bounced off their 200-day moving averages. Despite this technical strength, analysts point to high valuations and mixed fundamentals, questioning the long-term outlook.

item.title: India Luxury Homes: Lifestyle & Legacy Trump Speculation
item.description: The Indian luxury real estate market is undergoing a significant change. Affluent buyers are now prioritizing lifestyle enhancement and long-term wealth preservation over speculative short-term gains. Properties offering premium experiences, integrated services, and lasting value are in demand. Developers are increasingly focusing on ready-to-move-in projects to reduce construction risks and appeal to buyers seeking assets for future generations. While rental income is considered, it's secondary to the property's lifestyle benefits and value retention.

item.title: Jio IPO to Raise $4.3B As India Loosens Dilution Rules
item.description: Jio Platforms is preparing for what could be India's largest IPO, aiming to raise over $4.3 billion by selling just 2.5% of its shares. New regulations permit this minimal dilution for large companies. Despite valuations possibly reaching $170 billion, the IPO faces a subdued Indian market where recent listings have struggled, alongside global economic and geopolitical worries. Jio's rivals, Bharti Airtel and Vodafone Idea, are in very different positions as Jio benefits from its scale and supportive regulations.

item.title: Escorts Kubota Holds Investor Meeting; No Price-Sensitive Information Shared
item.description: Escorts Kubota Limited is scheduled to hold a one-on-one investor meeting with Japan Post Bank Co. Ltd. on March 20, 2026, in Faridabad. The company has confirmed that no unpublished price-sensitive information (UPSI) will be shared during this interaction, adhering to disclosure norms. The investor presentation is accessible on the company's website.

item.title: Omni Axs Software Taps Experienced Officer for Governance Push
item.description: Omni Axs Software Limited appointed Mrs. Lalita Jigar Shah as its new Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, effective March 16, 2026. Bringing over 12 years of experience, her role is set to enhance the company's corporate governance and regulatory compliance.

item.title: SEBI Approves 3 IPOs As 3 Firms Withdraw Amid Market Caution
item.description: India's Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) approved IPO plans for Travelstack Tech, Tea Post, and Learnfluence Education. In contrast, Rays Power Infra, Arjun Jewellers, and Madhur Iron & Steel India withdrew their proposed offerings. This shows a more selective primary market in early 2026, with investors carefully examining valuations, a shift from the booming IPO market of last year. Approved IPO funds will support expansion, debt repayment, and general corporate goals, while withdrawals highlight challenges from market volatility and global uncertainties.
